assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 33-39 contain a 'Deployment Context' section that explicitly states: 'The Source Repositories table in CLAUDE.md does **not** have a Deployment Context column. Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to `upstream`.' A table follows showing rhtpa-backend with 'upstream (default -- column absent)'. This demonstrates detection of the missing column and correct defaulting.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains two task descriptions (Preemptive Upstream Backport Task and Preemptive Downstream Propagation Subtask). Neither contains a 'Coordination Guidance' subsection. The subsections present are: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Dependencies. A full-text scan confirms 'Coordination Guidance' does not appear anywhere in the file.

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: Both output files (data-extraction.md and remediation.md) contain well-structured, complete outputs with no error messages, warnings, or indications of failure. data-extraction.md successfully completes CVE data parsing, stream scope resolution, ecosystem detection, deployment context handling, version impact analysis, upstream fix status, and affects versions correction. remediation.md produces a full triage outcome summary and generates appropriate preemptive remediation tasks for cross-stream impact (Case B). The missing Deployment Context column was handled gracefully via the default-to-upstream fallback without disrupting any step.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs demonstrate standard triage behavior: CVE data extraction is complete and correct, version impact analysis covers both streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x), stream scope resolution works, ecosystem detection identifies Cargo, and remediation produces properly structured tasks with correct labels (security-preemptive), link types (Related), and implementation details. The only adaptation for the missing column is the explicit default to 'upstream' (data-extraction.md line 35), which preserves the pre-column behavior. No functionality is degraded or missing compared to expected triage output.
